result:
Confirmed. Here's the complete answer.

## `struct list_head` value fields directly in `struct task_struct` body

`include/linux/sched.h`, struct spans lines 743–1554.

| # | Field | Line | Conditional? |
|---|-------|------|--------------|
| 1 | `rcu_node_entry` | 847 | `#ifdef CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU` |
| 2 | `rcu_tasks_holdout_list` | 856 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_RCU` |
| 3 | `trc_holdout_list` | 863 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| 4 | `trc_blkd_node` | 864 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| 5 | `tasks` | 870 | unconditional |
| 6 | `children` | 989 | unconditional |
| 7 | `sibling` | 990 | unconditional |
| 8 | `ptraced` | 999 | unconditional |
| 9 | `ptrace_entry` | 1000 | unconditional |
| 10 | `thread_group` | 1005 | unconditional |
| 11 | `thread_node` | 1006 | unconditional |
| 12 | `cg_list` | 1227 | `#ifdef CONFIG_CGROUPS` |
| 13 | `pi_state_list` | 1238 | `#ifdef CONFIG_FUTEX` (nested `CONFIG_COMPAT` block closes at line 1237, before this field — `pi_state_list` itself only depends on `CONFIG_FUTEX`) |
| 14 | `perf_event_list` | 1246 | `#ifdef CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS` |

**Total: 14 fields.**

**#ifdef treatment**: I counted every field regardless of guard — 6 unconditional and 8 gated (behind `CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU`, `CONFIG_TASKS_RCU`, `CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` ×2, `CONFIG_CGROUPS`, `CONFIG_FUTEX`, `CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS`). None were excluded for being conditional; the table states the exact config each needs.

**Unnamed struct/union**: none exist anywhere in `task_struct`'s body (verified: zero occurrences of a brace-opened anonymous `struct {`/`union {` between lines 743–1554), so no fields needed special handling for that case.

Cross-check: `grep -c "struct list_head[[:space:]]*[a-zA-Z_]"` over the same line range independently returns **14**, matching the table, and none of the matches carry a trailing `*` (confirming all are value fields, not pointers).

## Real iteration example

`kernel/exit.c:471`
```c
list_for_each_entry(c, &p->children, sibling) {
        if (c->mm == mm)
                goto assign_new_owner;
}
```
This walks `task_struct.children` (field #6, line 989) using the `sibling` field (#7, line 990) as the link — a real parent-of-`p` walking its child list in `mm_update_next_owner()`.
